events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a torque sequence violation during in-progress work. The torque sequence pattern (spiral/star from center) was violated. This is a sequence violation in threaded fastening work.

Key points:
- Work is in-progress
- Torque sequence pattern violated (spiral/star from center - SEQ)
- OEM/engineering specs are on hand
- Component condition: installed-defective (the fasteners/joint may be improperly torqued)

Decision: fail - sequence violation is non-compliant
Risk: high - improper torque sequence can lead to joint failure, uneven clamping force
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: installed-defective (fasteners installed with wrong sequence)
Percent complete: estimate ~50% since in-progress
Value: 0 (no specific numeric reading requested)
Confidence: high, maybe 0.9
Findings: torque sequence violated
Actions: stop work, back out fasteners, re-torque in correct sequence
Rationale: torque sequence must follow OEM spec spiral/star pattern from center
References: OEM/engineering specs
